Duguetia is a genus of flowering plants in the family Annonaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Duguetia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Duguetia is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and the most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, tonics, and salves.
